meta.pipeline: Progressive NLI (MNLI -> MedNLI -> ClaimGuard) + Conformal FDR
meta.note: Pod died during baseline phase; these are the results captured from log before disconnect. Training + eval complete. 2 baselines missing (DeBERTa NLI zero-shot, RadFlag).
meta.gpu_hours: ~2.5 (across 2 pods due to community cloud reclaim)

stage_1_mnli.status: completed on first pod (A100), checkpoint reused on second pod

v2_eval_conformal_fdr.model: DeBERTa-v3-large with progressive NLI + ClaimGuard fine-tuning

hypothesis_only.note: Training crashed at E3 due to pod disconnect. E1-E2 completed.
hypothesis_only.interpretation: SEVERE artifact signal: claim-only model hits 97.71% val acc without any evidence. This means synthetic perturbations (negation flip, laterality swap, etc.) contain strong lexical shortcuts detectable from claim text alone. Paper must acknowledge this limitation.

deberta_nli_zeroshot.reason: pod died before reaching this baseline

radflag_consistency.reason: pod died before reaching this baseline

key_findings: V2 DeBERTa-v3-large + progressive NLI outperforms v1 RoBERTa on validation accuracy (98.61% vs 98.31%)
V2 conformal FDR has higher power at every alpha level (99.32% vs 98.06% at alpha=0.05)
V2 FDR is slightly higher than v1 (1.66% vs 1.30% at alpha=0.05) but still well below target alpha
Hypothesis-only baseline reveals severe surface shortcut artifacts in synthetic hard negatives — this is a critical finding for the paper's limitations section
Pod instability on RunPod community cloud required 2 pods and a restart mechanism
